data.title: Mind Medicine prices $75M securities offering
data.description:
• Mind Medicine (NASDAQ:MNMD) has priced its previously announced securities offering to raise approximately $75M in gross proceeds.
  • The sales comprises 9,285,511 common shares, issued at a price of $7.00 per common share, and pre-funded warrants, offered to certain investors, to purchase 1,428,775 common shares at a price of $6.999 per pre-funded warrant.
  • MindMed (MNMD) intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to fund the research and development of its product candidates and working capital and general corporate purposes.

data.title: Future Fintech announces appointment of Hu Li as CEO
data.description:
• Future Fintech Group (NASDAQ:FTFT) on Friday announced that its Board of Directors approved the appointment of Hu Li as Chief Executive Officer, President and a member of the Board of the company, effective August 5.
  • Li replaces Shanchun Huang, who resigned as CEO for personal reasons.
  • Li has served as a director and CEO of FTFT International Securities and Futures Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of the company, since January 2024, and as Corporate Secretary of the company since June 2019.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Stock index futures reverse course, yields also inch lower
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Friday, a day after investors found some comfort from a bigger-than-expected decline in weekly initial jobless claims.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.4% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) down 5 basis points to at 3.94%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.03%.

Major U.S. stock indexes also ended higher on Thursday, as worries about the economy sliding into a recession were eased after the initial jobless claims came in less than expected.

"Barely a week since recessionary concerns rocked markets, investors have regained some poise, with share prices largely recouping the steep losses of the last few days," Richard Hunter, Interactive Investor said.

"Markets are closing out the week in a Disney paradise after having starting the week completely frozen! The buoyant market mood over the past 24 hours was given a significant boost by a decent U.S. jobless claims report," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The better outturn pointed to enough strength in the U.S. labor market not to suggest that it is not falling apart. It certainly provided some evidence to the view that part of the weak payrolls may have been weather-related effects, Reid added.

Traders were focused on the initial jobless claims report, as they looked to gauge the labor market after last week's disappointing jobs report.

The economic calendar is light today, with only the Baker-Hughes rig count on tap.

data.title: AMC Networks misses profit estimates widely for Q2, posts revenue beat
data.description:
AMC Networks (NASDAQ:AMCX) reported a top-line beat but missed on profit expectations widely for its second quarter results.

Shares of the company were -1.6% at $10.14 before the bell on Friday.

The company said streaming revenues rose 9% to $150M from last year, while streaming subscribers increased 5% to 11.6M.

Free cash flow for the company fell to $95.2M from $$147.6M in Q2, while operating income came in at $11M, which included impairment and other charges of $97M. Adjusted operating income was $153M, with a margin of 24%.

For the three months ended June 30, the company reported a net loss of $32M, or $0.66 loss per share, compared to a profit of $62.1 million, or $1.60 profit per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, it earned $1.24 per share, missing the average Wall Street analyst expectation by 41 cents.

Revenue fell nearly 8% to $625.9M but beat the consensus mark of $601.5M.

data.title: Blue Owl Capital Corp. cut to Perform at Raymond James on merger arb pressure
data.description:
Raymond James downgraded Blue Owl Capital Corp. (NYSE:OBDC) to Market Perform from Outperform as analyst Robert Dodd expects the firm's proposed merger with Blue Owl Capital Corp. III (NYSE:OBDE) may apply "potential direct technical pressure to OBDC through merger arbitrage channels."

"Overall, we expect OBDC to continue to demonstrate above-average realized NAV performance (as it has this cycle - up 1.6% this cycle, versus the industry at down 6.6%), and an attractive risk/reward given one of the more conservatively structured dividend + supplemental programs of the large BDC group," Dodd wrote in a note to clients.

However, technical issues over the next six or more months may offset the fundamental positives, leading to the downgrade, he added.

Dodd considers the proposed merger with OBDE to be fundamentally positive as it will result in increased scale, modest operating expense synergies, and a benefit to cost of incremental funding.

Raymond James' Market Perform rating aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating of Buy.

Blue Owl Capital Corp. (OBDE) stock dipped 0.6% in Friday premarket trading.

data.title: SoundHound AI upgraded following 'transformative acquisition': Cantor Fitzgerald
data.description:
SoundHound AI was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral by Cantor Fitzgerald as its acquisition of Amelia, an enterprise artificial intelligence software company, is expected to almost double its revenue in 2025.

SoundHound, which specializes in conversational AI solutions for businesses, announced the acquisition on Thursday, along with their second quarter 2024 financial results, which beat estimates.

With 2024 revenue expected to hit at least $80M, SoundHound now projects 2025 revenue to exceed $150M due to the acquisition. Amelia is slated to add $45M through recurring AI software revenue along with professional services and agent-related fees.

SoundHound shares surged 21% by the time markets closed Thursday. They were down slightly during pre-market trading on Friday.

Cantor Fitzgerald increased its price target to $7 from $5.

"We believe this could be a transformative acquisition for SOUN," said Cantor Ftizgeral analyst Brett Knoblauch, in a note.

"Amelia will add financial services and healthcare customer exposure to SOUN, and while they would have entered these markets, it likely would have taken them several years to do so," he added.

SoundHound acquired Amelia for $10M in cash, $70M in stock, and agreed to pay down $70M of Amelia's $100M debt.

"In other words, a purchase multiple of ~4.5x for a company that has meaningful recurring revenue and is operating across very synergistic end markets," Knoblauch said.

This follows the June acquisition announcement of Allset, a food ordering platform with nearly 7,000 restaurant partners in the U.S.

SoundHound revealed locking down a number of new deals with major clients. Its voice AI ordering system will be rolled out at Beef 'O' Bradys' in more than 20 states across the US. It also signed deals with a major pizza chain to handle phone ordering services. What's more, Employee Assist, SoundHound’s voice AI offering for back-of-house staff, will be utilized by two coffee shop chains.

SoundHound expanded its partnership with Stellantis (STLA) to six brands.

SoundHound has a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha analysts, but a Buy rating from Wall Street analysts. It has a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha's Quant system, which routinely beats the market.

data.title: LANXESS Aktiengesellschaft reports Q2 results; initiates Q3 and reaffirms FY24 outlook
data.description:
• LANXESS Aktiengesellschaft press release (OTCPK:LNXSF): Q2 EBITDA pre exceptionals up 69.2 percent at €181 million.

  • Revenue of €1.68B (-5.6% Y/Y).

  • LANXESS is confirming its forecast for the current fiscal year 2024 and continues to anticipate an increase in earnings of 10 to 20 percent compared to the previous year’s figure of €512 million EBITDA pre exceptionals. For the third quarter of 2024, LANXESS expects EBITDA pre exceptionals to be close to or up to the level of the second quarter. For the fourth quarter, the Group then anticipates a more muted performance as a result of the normal seasonal development.
